statement_notes: economic activity expanding at a solid pace despite elevated uncertainty partly owed to the Middle East conflict
inflation remains elevated relative to the 2 percent goal partly reflecting supply shocks including energy

text: The FOMC maintained the federal funds target range at 3.50%-3.75% on June 17, 2026 by a 12-0 vote, while stating that economic activity was expanding at a solid pace despite elevated uncertainty partly owed to the Middle East conflict and that inflation remained elevated partly because of supply shocks including energy.

calculation.method: counted 2026 federal-funds dots above the current 3.625 percent target-range midpoint and read median projection table

text: The June 2026 FOMC projection materials showed a 3.8% median end-2026 federal funds projection, with 9 of 18 June participants placing their 2026 dot above the current 3.625% midpoint, and median 2026 PCE/core PCE inflation projections of 3.6% and 3.3%.

calculation.method: Treasury 10Y par yield minus 2Y par yield, compared with the prior Treasury business day

text: The official U.S. Treasury par-yield curve on June 18, 2026 showed the 2-year yield at 4.19%, the 10-year at 4.46%, and the 30-year at 4.90%, with the 2s10s spread flattening to +27 basis points from +29 basis points on June 17.

subject.name: Bank of England Monetary Policy Committee

text: The Bank of England kept Bank Rate at 3.75% on June 18, 2026 by a 7-2 vote, with Megan Greene and Huw Pill preferring a 25 bp increase to 4.00%; the minutes put May CPI at 2.8%, projected CPI a little under 3% in Q3 and a little over 3.25% in Q4, and noted Brent around $79 and UK wholesale gas around 100 pence per therm immediately before the meeting.

The Swiss National Bank left its policy rate at 0.00% on June 18, 2026, kept the discount on sight deposits above the threshold at 0.25 percentage points, said May inflation had risen to 0.6% from 0.1% in February, and forecast annual inflation of 0.6% in 2026, 0.6% in 2027, and 0.7% in 2028 under a 0% policy-rate assumption while noting increased willingness to intervene in FX if necessary.

The U.S. Department of Labor reported June 18 that seasonally adjusted initial jobless claims fell by 4,000 to 226,000 for the week ending June 13, 2026, while the four-week average rose to 223,250 and insured unemployment increased by 24,000 to 1.810 million for the week ending June 6 with the insured unemployment rate unchanged at 1.2%.
Trading Economics reported on June 18, 2026 that DXY increased to 100.72, its highest level since May 2025, with the Dollar Index up 1.39% over four weeks and 1.83% over 12 months.

On June 18, 2026, NYMEX WTI for July delivery settled at $76.60/bbl, down $0.19 or 0.3% after a $73.58 intraday low, while August ICE Brent settled at $79.85/bbl, down $0.30 or 0.4% after a $76.54 intraday low.

value.us_blockade_status: lifted on Iranian ports on the strait

text: DTN reported June 18 that the U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding appeared to assure immediate toll-free transit through Hormuz, a route that normally oversees about 20 million bpd of petroleum liquids, and that the U.S. military had lifted its naval blockade on Iranian ports on the strait while Goldman Sachs projected a 13 million bpd surge in transit flows and full normalization by late July.
